formations_blocks: Panama short-pass possession in Ghana’s half
Ghana direct long-ball play toward Semenyo
Panama attacking block with crosses and forward balls
Ghana largely passive/without sustained possession, occasionally fouling
adjustments: Ghana repeatedly trying a direct route by blasting the ball forward for Semenyo
Panama mixing short possession with longer balls into Waterman/Murillo
phases.summary: Panama controlled the opening spell, combining short passing with direct entries and crosses that repeatedly put Ghana under pressure. Ghana were mostly reduced to clearances, fouls, and hopeful balls forward, with their main attacking outlet being direct service toward Semenyo or Nuamah on the right.

formations_blocks: Panama possession with patient circulation through the back line
Ghana brief pressing spells leading into a more active press after the hydration break
Panama forced into a deeper defensive block as Ghana gain territory
adjustments: Hydration break appears to have shifted momentum toward Ghana
Ghana increase pressing intensity and force turnovers higher up the pitch
Panama’s forward progress drops off and they circulate possession more cautiously
key_matchups: Ati-Zigi and a Ghanaian teammate vs a Panamanian attacker
phases.summary: The opening part of the phase is dominated by Panama’s controlled possession, but much of it is lateral and in front of Ghana’s shape, with little penetration. After the hydration break, Ghana become more aggressive without the ball, press higher, and start forcing Panama into longer or aimless passes, swinging momentum toward a more even contest.

formations_blocks: Panama with a structured attacking pattern: two players wide, Waterman central, and two midfielders close to the penalty box
Ghana with an unclear attacking structure, lacking a clearly identifiable forward pattern
adjustments: Ghana need to sort out their sputtering attack at halftime
Lawrence Ati-Zigi was replaced at halftime by Benjamin Asare
key_matchups: Lawrence Ati-Zigi vs Panama’s early pressure/crosses
Brandon Thomas-Asante as a central attacking runner vs Panama’s defensive shape
phases.summary: Panama looked the more organized side in the first half, with a clear attacking pattern and far more of the ball, completing 277 passes to Ghana’s 149. Ghana, by contrast, were struggling to define their front line or attacking structure, while Ati-Zigi’s early save and cross-claiming helped keep the game level before his halftime departure.

formations_blocks: Panama passive possession with a back line circulating the ball
adjustments: Ghana become slightly more potent in attack after the goalkeeper change
Ghana introduce Fatawu and Thomas-Asante for Sulemana and Nuamah at 58'
Panama continue to look long and struggle for passing sharpness
Panama make changes at 63' with Waterman and Martinez going off
key_matchups: Rodriguez vs multiple Ghana defenders on the left flank
Adjetey vs Panama on the 48' set piece
phases.summary: The second half starts with an enforced Ghana goalkeeper change, but the new keeper’s arrival is followed by a modest uptick in Ghana’s attacking threat, especially from set pieces and direct balls. Panama remain largely passive in possession and their passing loses sharpness, while Ghana’s substitutions add pace up front and the game settles into tense midfield duels and occasional transition moments.

formations_blocks: Panama abandoning the press and allowing Ghana to circulate the ball
Panama's defensive block becomes more reactive, focusing on aggressive blocking and disruption
Ghana spending long spells in Panama's half without much penetration
adjustments: Owusu off, Sibo on for Ghana
Ayew off, Prince Adu on for Ghana
Panama sub: Blackman off, Godoy on
Panama's pressing approach drops off noticeably after 80'
key_matchups: Murillo squaring up with a defender
Diaz vs Asare on the 85' shot
Thomas-Asante's late run/action denied by offside
phases.summary: This phase saw Panama abandon their press, which let Ghana keep the ball more easily, but Ghana still struggled to turn possession into clear chances. Panama's defense became increasingly aggressive at blocking and disrupting, while their own passing deteriorated and counterattacks fizzled out. The decisive moment came late when Ghana finally forced a big save, only for the flag to go up against Thomas-Asante, before Ghana ultimately struck in stoppage time through Yirenkyi.
